3: ##
4: # The Provider class represents an abstract class for
5: # LLM (Language Model) providers.
6: #
7: # @abstract
8: class LLM::Provider
9:   include LLM::Transport::Execution
10: 
11:   ##
12:   # @param [String, nil] key
13:   #  The secret key for authentication
14:   # @param [String] host
15:   #  The host address of the LLM provider
16:   # @param [Integer] port
17:   #  The port number
18:   # @param [Integer] timeout
19:   #  The number of seconds to wait for a response
20:   # @param [Boolean] ssl
21:   #  Whether to use SSL for the connection
22:   # @param [String] base_path
23:   #  Optional base path prefix for HTTP API routes.
24:   # @param [Boolean] persistent
25:   #  Whether to use a persistent connection.
26:   #  Requires the net-http-persistent gem.
27:   # @param [LLM::Transport, Class, nil] transport
28:   #  Optional override with any {LLM::Transport} instance or subclass.
29:   def initialize(key:, host:, port: 443, timeout: 60, ssl: true, base_path: "", persistent: false, transport: nil)
30:     @key = key
31:     @host = host
32:     @port = port
33:     @timeout = timeout
34:     @ssl = ssl
35:     @base_path = LLM::Utils.normalize_base_path(base_path)
36:     @base_uri = URI("#{ssl ? "https" : "http"}://#{host}:#{port}/")
37:     @headers = {"User-Agent" => "llm.rb v#{LLM::VERSION}"}
38:     @transport = LLM::Transport::Utils.resolve_transport(host:, port:, timeout:, ssl:, transport:, persistent:)
39:     @monitor = Monitor.new
40:   end
41: 
42:   ##
43:   # Returns an inspection of the provider object
44:   # @return [String]
45:   # @note The secret key is redacted in inspect for security reasons
46:   def inspect
47:     "#<#{LLM::Utils.object_id(self)} @key=[REDACTED] @transport=#{transport.inspect} @tracer=#{tracer.inspect}>"
48:   end

51:   # @raise [NotImplementedError]
52:   #  When the method is not implemented by a subclass
53:   # @return [Symbol]
54:   #  Returns the provider's name
55:   def name
56:     raise NotImplementedError
57:   end

3: ##
4: # The {LLM::Registry LLM::Registry} class provides a small API over
5: # provider model data. It exposes model metadata such as pricing,
6: # capabilities, modalities, and limits from the registry files
7: # stored under `data/`. The data is provided by https://models.dev
8: # and shipped with llm.rb.
9: class LLM::Registry
10:   @root = File.join(__dir__, "..", "..")
11: 
12:   ##
13:   # @raise [LLM::Error]
14:   #  Might raise an error
15:   # @param [Symbol]
16:   #  A provider name
17:   # @return [LLM::Registry]
18:   def self.for(name)
19:     path = File.join @root, "data", "#{name}.json"
20:     if File.file?(path)
21:       new LLM.json.load(File.binread(path))
22:     else
23:       raise LLM::NoSuchRegistryError, "no registry found for #{name}"
24:     end
25:   end
26: 
27:   ##
28:   # @param [Hash] blob
29:   #  A model registry
30:   # @return [LLM::Registry]
31:   def initialize(blob)
32:     @registry = LLM::Object.from(blob)
33:     @models = @registry.models
34:   end
35: 
36:   ##
37:   # @return [LLM::Object]
38:   #  Returns model costs
39:   def cost(model:)
40:     lookup(model:).cost
41:   end
42: 
43:   ##
44:   # @return [LLM::Object]
45:   #  Returns model modalities
46:   def modalities(model:)
47:     lookup(model:).modalities
48:   end
49: 
50:   ##
51:   # @return [LLM::Object]
52:   #  Returns model limits such as the context window size
53:   def limit(model:)
54:     lookup(model:).limit
55:   end
56: 
57:   private
58: 
59:   def lookup(model:)
60:     if @models.key?(model)
61:       @models[model]
62:     else
63:       patterns = {/-\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/ => "", /\A(gpt-.*)-\d{4}$/ => "\\1"}
64:       fallback = find_map(patterns) { model.dup.sub!(_1, _2) } || "none"
65:       if @models.key?(fallback)
66:         @models[fallback]
67:       else
68:         raise LLM::NoSuchModelError, "no such model: #{model} (fallback: #{fallback})"
69:       end
70:     end
71:   end
72: 
73:   ##
74:   # Similar to #{find} but returns the block's return value
75:   # @return [Object, nil]
76:   def find_map(pair)
77:     result = nil
78:     pair.each_pair { break if result = yield(_1, _2) }
79:     result
80:   end
81: end

17: 18: ## About 19: 20: llm.rb is Ruby's most capable AI runtime. 21: 22: It runs on Ruby's standard library by default. loads optional pieces 23: only when needed, and offers a single runtime for providers, agents, 24: tools, skills, MCP, A2A (Agent2Agent), RAG (vector stores & embeddings), 25: streaming, files, and persisted state. 26: 27: It supports OpenAI, OpenAI-compatible endpoints, Anthropic, Google 28: Gemini, DeepSeek, xAI, Z.ai, AWS Bedrock, Ollama, and llama.cpp. It 29: also includes built-in ActiveRecord and Sequel support, plus concurrent 30: tool execution through threads, tasks (via async gem), fibers, ractors, 31: and fork (via xchan.rb gem). 32: 33: **See also:** 34: 35: * [mruby-llm](https://github.com/llmrb/mruby-llm) - the llm.rb runtime for mruby 36: * [wasm-llm](https://github.com/llmrb/wasm-llm) - the llm.rb runtime ported to WebAssembly 37: * [rails-llm](https://github.com/llmrb/rails-llm) - the llm.rb runtime optimized for Rails 38: * [robert](https://github.com/llmrb/robert) - FreeBSD teacher as a 3MB, statically linked binary 39: 40: ## Quick start 41: 42: #### LLM::Context 43: 44: The 45: [LLM::Context](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Context.html) 46: object is at the heart of the runtime. Almost all other features build 47: on top of it. It is a low-level interface to a model, and requires tool 48: execution to be managed manually. The 49: [LLM::Agent](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Agent.html) 50: class is almost the same as 51: [LLM::Context](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Context.html) 52: but it manages tool execution for you - we'll cover agents next: 53: 54: ```ruby 55: require "llm" 56: 57: llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"]) 58: ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm, stream: $stdout) 59: ctx.talk "Hello world" 60: ``` 61: 62: #### LLM::Agent 63: 64: The 65: [LLM::Agent](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Agent.html) 66: object is implemented on top of 67: [LLM::Context](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Context.html). 68: It provides the same interface, but manages tool execution for you. It 69: also has builtin features such as a loop guard that detects repeated 70: tool call patterns, and another guard that detects infinite tool call 71: loops. Both guards advise the model to change course rather than raise 72: an error: 73: 74: ```ruby 75: require "llm" 76: 77: llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"]) 78: agent = LLM::Agent.new(llm, stream: $stdout) 79: agent.talk "Hello world" 80: ``` 81: 82: #### Agents (Advanced) 83: 84: An agent can be configured to require confirmation before a tool is 85: executed. When a matching tool is called, llm.rb runs 86: `on_tool_confirmation`. That callback must decide whether to cancel the 87: tool call or approve it and execute it by calling 88: `fn.spawn(strategy).wait`, and it must always return an instance of 89: [`LLM::Function::Return`](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Function/Return.html): 90: 91: ```ruby 92: require "llm" 93: 94: class Agent < LLM::Agent 95: tools DeleteFile 96: confirm "delete-file" 97: 98: def on_tool_confirmation(fn, strategy) 99: path = fn.arguments.path 100: if path.start_with?("/tmp/") 101: fn.spawn(strategy).wait 102: else 103: fn.cancel(reason: "Deletion requires approval") 104: end 105: end 106: end 107: 108: llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"]) 109: Agent.new(llm, stream: $stdout).talk("Delete /tmp/example.txt.") 110: ``` 111: 112: #### Tools 113: 114: The 115: [LLM::Tool](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Tool.html) 116: class can be subclassed to implement your own tools that can extend the 117: abilities of a model: 118: 119: ```ruby 120: class ReadFile < LLM::Tool 121: name "read-file" 122: description "Read a file" 123: parameter :path, String, "The filename or path" 124: required %i[path] 125: 126: def call(path:) 127: {contents: File.read(path)} 128: end 129: end 130: ``` 131: 132: #### MCP 133: 134: The 135: [LLM::MCP](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/MCP.html) 136: object lets llm.rb use tools provided by an MCP server. Those tools are 137: exposed through the same runtime as local tools, so you can pass them 138: to either 139: [LLM::Context](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Context.html) 140: or 141: [LLM::Agent](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Agent.html). 142: In this example, the MCP server runs over stdio and 143: [LLM::Agent](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Agent.html) 144: manages the tool loop. For **stdio**, `mcp.session` is the preferred 145: pattern because it keeps one MCP session alive across discovery and 146: tool calls: 147: 148: ```ruby 149: require "llm" 150: 151: llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"]) 152: mcp = LLM::MCP.stdio(argv: ["ruby", "server.rb"]) 153: 154: mcp.session do 155: agent = LLM::Agent.new(llm, stream: $stdout, tools: mcp.tools) 156: agent.talk "Use the available tools to inspect the environment." 157: end 158: ``` 159: 160: MCP can also be used without `session`. Although it works it is generally 161: not recommended for the **stdio** transport because it is inefficient 162: to start and stop a fresh MCP process for tool discovery and each tool 163: call: 164: 165: ```ruby 166: require "llm" 167: 168: llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"]) 169: mcp = LLM::MCP.stdio(argv: ["ruby", "server.rb"]) 170: 171: agent = LLM::Agent.new(llm, tools: mcp.tools) 172: agent.talk("Use the available tools to inspect the environment.") 173: ``` 174: 175: The HTTP transport can be used with or without the `session` method, 176: and unlike the stdio transport it can remain efficient without the 177: `session` method through a persistent connection pool that is available 178: through the 179: [LLM::Transport.net_http_persistent](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Transport.html#method-c-net_http_persistent) 180: transport: 181: 182: ```ruby 183: require "llm" 184: 185: llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"]) 186: mcp = LLM::MCP.http( 187: url: "https://remote-mcp.example.com", 188: transport: :net_http_persistent 189: ) 190: 191: agent = LLM::Agent.new(llm, tools: mcp.tools) 192: agent.talk("Use the available tools to inspect the environment.") 193: ``` 194: 195: #### A2A (Agent 2 Agent) 196: 197: The 198: [LLM::A2A](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/A2A.html) 199: object lets llm.rb use skills provided by a remote A2A agent. Those 200: skills are exposed through the same runtime as local tools, so you can 201: pass them to either 202: [LLM::Context](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Context.html) 203: or 204: [LLM::Agent](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Agent.html). 205: 206: Use remote skills as local tools: 207: 208: ```ruby 209: require "llm" 210: 211: a2a = LLM::A2A.rest( 212: url: "https://remote-agent.example.com", 213: headers: {"Authorization" => "Bearer token"} 214: ) 215: llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"]) 216: agent = LLM::Agent.new(llm, tools: a2a.skills) 217: agent.talk "Analyze this CSV and summarize the trends." 218: ``` 219: 220: Use persistent HTTP connections: 221: 222: ```ruby 223: require "llm" 224: 225: a2a = LLM::A2A.rest( 226: url: "https://remote-agent.example.com", 227: transport: :net_http_persistent 228: ) 229: ``` 230: 231: For more on direct messaging, task operations, push notification 232: configs, and JSON-RPC, see the 233: [LLM::A2A API docs](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/A2A.html). 234: 235: #### Transports 236: 237: Providers use Ruby's standard library Net::HTTP transport by default. 238: You can opt into persistent Net::HTTP connections with `persistent: true`, 239: or provide a transport shortcut when you want a different backend. 240: `transport: :curb` uses libcurl through the optional `curb` gem. 241: 242: Custom transports can implement the 243: [LLM::Transport](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Transport.html) 244: interface and receive transport-agnostic 245: [LLM::Transport::Request](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Transport/Request.html) 246: objects from providers. 247: 248: ```ruby 249: require "llm" 250: 251: llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"], persistent: true) 252: llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"], transport: :net_http_persistent) 253: llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"], transport: :curb) 254: ``` 255: 256: #### Skills 257: 258: Skills are reusable instructions loaded from a `SKILL.md` directory. They let 259: you package behavior and tool access together, and they plug into the 260: same runtime as tools, agents, MCP, and A2A. When a skill runs, llm.rb 261: spawns a subagent with the skill instructions, access to only the tools 262: listed in the skill, and recent conversation context: 263: 264: ```yaml 265: --- 266: name: release 267: description: Prepare a release 268: tools: ["search-docs", "git"] 269: --- 270: 271: ## Task 272: 273: Review the release state, summarize what changed, and prepare the release. 274: ``` 275: 276: ```ruby 277: require "llm" 278: 279: class ReleaseAgent < LLM::Agent 280: model "gpt-5.4-mini" 281: skills "./skills/release" 282: end 283: 284: llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"]) 285: ReleaseAgent.new(llm, stream: $stdout).talk("Prepare the next release.") 286: ``` 287: 288: A skill can also have its sub-agent inherit the parents tools through the 289: `inherit` directive. The `inherit` directive has coverage for the "classic" 290: tools (a subclass of [LLM::Tool](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Tool.html)), 291: MCP tools, and A2A tools that a parent context or agent has access to: 292: 293: ```yaml 294: --- 295: name: release 296: description: Prepare a release 297: tools: inherit 298: --- 299: ``` 300: 301: #### LLM::Stream 302: 303: The 304: [LLM::Stream](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Stream.html) 305: object lets you observe output and runtime events as they happen. You 306: can subclass it to handle streamed content in your own application: 307: 308: ```ruby 309: require "llm" 310: 311: class Stream < LLM::Stream 312: def on_content(content) 313: $stdout << content 314: end 315: end 316: 317: llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"]) 318: agent = LLM::Agent.new(llm, stream: Stream.new) 319: agent.talk "Write a haiku about Ruby." 320: ``` 321: 322: #### LLM::Stream (advanced) 323: 324: The 325: [LLM::Stream](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Stream.html) 326: object can also resolve tool calls while output is still streaming. In 327: `on_tool_call`, you can spawn the tool, push the work onto the stream 328: queue, and later drain it with `wait`: 329: 330: ```ruby 331: require "llm" 332: 333: class Stream < LLM::Stream 334: def on_content(content) 335: $stdout << content 336: end 337: 338: def on_tool_call(tool, error) 339: return queue << error if error 340: queue << ctx.spawn(tool, :thread) 341: end 342: end 343: 344: llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"]) 345: ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm, stream: Stream.new, tools: [ReadFile]) 346: ctx.talk "Read README.md and summarize the quick start." 347: ctx.talk(ctx.wait) while ctx.functions? 348: ``` 349: 350: #### Concurrency 351: 352: llm.rb can run tool work concurrently. This is useful when a model calls 353: multiple tools and you want to resolve them in parallel instead of one 354: at a time. On 355: [LLM::Agent](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Agent.html), 356: you can enable this with `concurrency`. Common options are `:call` for 357: sequential execution, `:thread`, or `:task` for concurrent IO-bound work, and 358: `:ractor` or `:fork` for more isolated CPU-bound work: 359: 360: ```ruby 361: require "llm" 362: 363: class Agent < LLM::Agent 364: model "gpt-5.4-mini" 365: tools ReadFile 366: concurrency :thread 367: end 368: 369: llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"]) 370: agent = Agent.new(llm, stream: $stdout) 371: agent.talk "Read README.md and CHANGELOG.md and compare them." 372: ``` 373: 374: #### Serialization 375: 376: The [`LLM::Agent`](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Agent.html) 377: object can be serialized to JSON, which makes it suitable for storing 378: in a file, a database column, or a Redis queue. The built-in 379: ActiveRecord and Sequel plugins are built on top of the same underlying 380: serialization feature: 381: 382: ```ruby 383: require "llm" 384: 385: llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"]) 386: 387: # Serialize an agent 388: agent1 = LLM::Agent.new(llm) 389: agent1.talk "Remember that my favorite language is Ruby" 390: string = agent1.to_json 391: 392: # Restore an agent (from JSON) 393: agent2 = LLM::Agent.new(llm, stream: $stdout) 394: agent2.restore(string:) 395: agent2.talk "What is my favorite language?" 396: ``` 397: 398: #### ask 399: 400: [`LLM::Agent`](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Agent.html) 401: also provides `ask`, a convenience interface that is compatible with 402: RubyLLM's `ask` method. It accepts a prompt, an optional `with:` 403: attachment path or paths, an optional `stream:` target, and an optional 404: block that chunks are yielded to. It returns an 405: [`LLM::Response`](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Response.html), 406: so use `.content` when you want the text directly: 407: 408: ```ruby 409: require "llm" 410: 411: llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"]) 412: agent = LLM::Agent.new(llm) 413: 414: puts agent.ask("Hello world").content 415: puts agent.ask("Summarize this document.", with: "README.md").content 416: agent.ask("Stream this reply.") { $stdout << _1 } 417: ``` 418: 419: ## Installation 420: 421: ```bash 422: gem install llm.rb 423: ``` 424: 425: ## Examples 426: 427: #### REPL 428: 429: This example uses [`LLM::Agent`](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Agent.html) 430: for an interactive REPL.
See the 431: [deepdive (web)](https://llmrb.github.io/llm.rb/) or 432: [deepdive (markdown)](resources/deepdive.md) for more examples. 433: 434: ```ruby 435: require "llm" 436: 437: llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"]) 438: agent = LLM::Agent.new(llm, stream: $stdout) 439: 440: loop do 441: print "> " 442: agent.talk(STDIN.gets || break) 443: puts 444: end 445: ``` 446: 447: #### Multimodal: Local Files 448: 449: In llm.rb, a prompt can be a string, an [`LLM::Prompt`](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Prompt.html), or an array. 450: When you use an array, each element can be plain text or a tagged object such as 451: [`agent.image_url(...)`](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Agent.html#image_url-instance_method), 452: [`agent.local_file(...)`](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Agent.html#local_file-instance_method), 453: or [`agent.remote_file(...)`](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Agent.html#remote_file-instance_method). 454: Those tagged objects carry the metadata the provider adapter needs to turn one 455: Ruby prompt into the provider-specific multimodal request schema. 456: 457: If the model understands that file type, you can attach a local file directly 458: with `agent.ask(..., with: path)` instead of uploading it first through a 459: provider Files API. Under the hood, llm.rb tags the path as a 460: [`agent.local_file(...)`](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Agent.html#local_file-instance_method) 461: object: 462: 463: ```ruby 464: require "llm" 465: 466: llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"]) 467: agent = LLM::Agent.new(llm) 468: puts agent.ask("Summarize this document.", with: "README.md").content 469: ``` 470: 471: #### Context Compaction 472: 473: This example uses [`LLM::Agent`](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Agent.html), 474: [`LLM::Compactor`](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Compactor.html), and 475: [`LLM::Stream`](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Stream.html) together so 476: long-lived conversations can summarize older history and expose the lifecycle 477: through stream hooks. This approach is inspired by General Intelligence 478: Systems. The 479: compactor can also use its own `model:` if you want summarization to run on a 480: different model from the main conversation. `token_threshold:` accepts either a 481: fixed token count or a percentage string like `"90%"`, which resolves 482: against the active model context window and triggers compaction once total 483: token usage goes over that percentage. See the 484: [deepdive (web)](https://llmrb.github.io/llm.rb/) or 485: [deepdive (markdown)](resources/deepdive.md) for more examples. 486: 487: ```ruby 488: require "llm" 489: 490: class Stream < LLM::Stream 491: def on_compaction(ctx, compactor) 492: puts "Compacting #{ctx.messages.size} messages..." 493: end 494: 495: def on_compaction_finish(ctx, compactor) 496: puts "Compacted to #{ctx.messages.size} messages." 497: end 498: end 499: 500: llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"]) 501: agent = LLM::Agent.new( 502: llm, 503: stream: Stream.new, 504: compactor: { 505: token_threshold: "90%", 506: retention_window: 8, 507: model: "gpt-5.4-mini" 508: } 509: ) 510: ``` 511: 512: #### Reasoning 513: 514: This example uses [`LLM::Stream`](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Stream.html) 515: with the OpenAI Responses API so reasoning output is streamed separately from 516: visible assistant output. See the 517: [deepdive (web)](https://llmrb.github.io/llm.rb/) or 518: [deepdive (markdown)](resources/deepdive.md) for more examples. 519: 520: To use the Responses API (OpenAI-specific), initialize an agent with 521: `mode: :responses` and keep using `talk` for turns. 522: 523: ```ruby 524: require "llm" 525: 526: class Stream < LLM::Stream 527: def on_content(content) 528: $stdout << content 529: end 530: 531: def on_reasoning_content(content) 532: $stderr << content 533: end 534: end 535: 536: llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"]) 537: agent = LLM::Agent.new( 538: llm, 539: model: "gpt-5.4-mini", 540: mode: :responses, 541: reasoning: {effort: "medium"}, 542: stream: Stream.new 543: ) 544: agent.talk("Solve 17 * 19 and show your work.") 545: ``` 546: 547: #### Request Cancellation 548: 549: Need to cancel a stream? llm.rb has you covered through 550: [`LLM::Agent#interrupt!`](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Agent.html#interrupt-21-instance_method). 551:
See the [deepdive (web)](https://llmrb.github.io/llm.rb/) 552: or [deepdive (markdown)](resources/deepdive.md) for more examples. 553: 554: ```ruby 555: require "llm" 556: require "io/console" 557: 558: llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"]) 559: agent = LLM::Agent.new(llm, stream: $stdout) 560: worker = Thread.new do 561: agent.talk("Write a very long essay about network protocols.") 562: rescue LLM::Interrupt 563: puts "Request was interrupted!" 564: end 565: 566: STDIN.getch 567: agent.interrupt! 568: worker.join 569: ``` 570: 571: #### Sequel (ORM) 572: 573: The `plugin :llm` integration wraps 574: [`LLM::Context`](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Context.html) on a 575: `Sequel::Model` and keeps tool execution explicit. Like the ActiveRecord 576: wrappers, its built-in persistence contract is the serialized `data` column, 577: while `provider:` resolves a real `LLM::Provider` instance and `context:` 578: injects defaults such as `model:`.
See the 579: [deepdive (web)](https://llmrb.github.io/llm.rb/) or 580: [deepdive (markdown)](resources/deepdive.md) for more examples. 581: 582: ```ruby 583: require "llm" 584: require "net/http/persistent" 585: require "sequel" 586: require "sequel/plugins/llm" 587: 588: class Context < Sequel::Model 589: plugin :llm, provider: :set_provider, context: :set_context 590: 591: private 592: 593: def set_provider 594: LLM.openai(key: ENV["OPENAI_SECRET"], persistent: true) 595: end 596: 597: def set_context 598: {model: "gpt-5.4-mini", mode: :responses, store: false} 599: end 600: end 601: 602: ctx = Context.create 603: ctx.talk("Remember that my favorite language is Ruby") 604: puts ctx.talk("What is my favorite language?").content 605: ``` 606: 607: #### ActiveRecord (ORM): acts_as_llm 608: 609: The `acts_as_llm` method wraps [`LLM::Context`](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Context.html) and 610: provides full control over tool execution. Its built-in persistence contract is 611: one serialized `data` column. If your app has provider, model, or usage 612: columns, provide them to llm.rb through `provider:` and `context:` instead of 613: relying on reserved wrapper columns. 614: 615: See the [deepdive (web)](https://llmrb.github.io/llm.rb/) 616: or [deepdive (markdown)](resources/deepdive.md) for more examples. 617: 618: ```ruby 619: require "llm" 620: require "active_record" 621: require "llm/active_record" 622: 623: class Context < ApplicationRecord 624: acts_as_llm provider: :set_provider, context: :set_context 625: 626: private 627: 628: def set_provider 629: LLM.openai(key: ENV["OPENAI_SECRET"]) 630: end 631: 632: def set_context 633: {model: "gpt-5.4-mini", mode: :responses, store: false} 634: end 635: end 636: 637: ctx = Context.create! 638: ctx.talk("Remember that my favorite language is Ruby") 639: puts ctx.talk("What is my favorite language?").content 640: ``` 641: 642: ```ruby 643: require "llm" 644: require "active_record" 645: require "llm/active_record" 646: 647: class Context < ApplicationRecord 648: acts_as_llm provider: :set_provider, context: :set_context 649: 650: # Optional application columns can still provide the provider and context. 651: # For example, `provider_name` and `model_name` can be normal columns. 652: 653: private 654: 655: def set_provider 656: LLM.public_send(provider_name, key: provider_key) 657: end 658: 659: def set_context 660: {model: model_name, mode: :responses, store: false} 661: end 662: end 663: ``` 664: 665: #### ActiveRecord (ORM): acts_as_agent 666: 667: The `acts_as_agent` method wraps [`LLM::Agent`](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/Agent.html) and 668: manages tool execution for you. Like `acts_as_llm`, its built-in persistence 669: contract is one serialized `data` column. If your app has provider or model 670: columns, provide them to llm.rb through your hooks and agent DSL. 671: 672: See the [deepdive (web)](https://llmrb.github.io/llm.rb/) 673: or [deepdive (markdown)](resources/deepdive.md) for more examples. 674: 675: ```ruby 676: require "llm" 677: require "active_record" 678: require "llm/active_record" 679: 680: class Ticket < ApplicationRecord 681: acts_as_agent provider: :set_provider, context: :set_context 682: model "gpt-5.4-mini" 683: instructions "You are a concise support assistant." 684: tools SearchDocs, Escalate 685: concurrency :thread 686: 687: private 688: 689: def set_provider 690: LLM.openai(key: ENV["OPENAI_SECRET"]) 691: end 692: 693: def set_context 694: {mode: :responses, store: false} 695: end 696: end 697: 698: ticket = Ticket.create! 699: puts ticket.talk("How do I rotate my API key?").content 700: ``` 701: 702: ```ruby 703: require "llm" 704: require "active_record" 705: require "llm/active_record" 706: 707: class Ticket < ApplicationRecord 708: acts_as_agent provider: :set_provider, context: :set_context 709: model "gpt-5.4-mini" 710: instructions "You are a concise support assistant." 711: 712: private 713: 714: def set_provider 715: LLM.public_send(provider_name, key: provider_key) 716: end 717: 718: def set_context 719: {mode: :responses, store: false} 720: end 721: end 722: ``` 723: 724: #### MCP 725: 726: This example uses [`LLM::MCP`](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb/LLM/MCP.html) 727: over HTTP so remote GitHub MCP tools run through the same 728: `LLM::Agent` tool path as local tools. It expects a GitHub token in 729: `ENV["GITHUB_PAT"]`. See the 730: [deepdive (web)](https://llmrb.github.io/llm.rb/) or 731: [deepdive (markdown)](resources/deepdive.md) for more examples. 732: 733: ```ruby 734: require "llm" 735: require "net/http/persistent" 736: 737: llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"], persistent: true) 738: mcp = LLM::MCP.http( 739: url: "https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp/", 740: headers: {"Authorization" => "Bearer #{ENV["GITHUB_PAT"]}"}, 741: persistent: true 742: ) 743: 744: agent = LLM::Agent.new(llm, stream: $stdout, tools: mcp.tools) 745: agent.talk("Pull information about my GitHub account.") 746: ``` 747: 748: ## Resources 749: 750: - [deepdive (web)](https://llmrb.github.io/llm.rb/) and 751: [deepdive (markdown)](resources/deepdive.md) are the examples guide. 752: - [relay](https://github.com/llmrb/relay) shows a real application built on 753: top of llm.rb. 754: - [doc site](https://0x1eef.github.io/x/llm.rb?rebuild=1) has the API docs. 755: 756: ## License 757: 758: [BSD Zero Clause](https://choosealicense.com/licenses/0bsd/) 759:

171:     ##
172:     # A convenience method for performing a web search using the
173:     # OpenAI web search tool.

178:     # @param query [String] The search query.

180:     def web_search(query:)

183:         type: :web_search

212:       tools = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
213:       params = [params, adapt_schema(params), adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
214:       role, stream = params.delete(:role), params.delete(:stream)
215:       params[:stream] = true if streamable?(stream) || stream == true
216:       if params[:stream]
217:         params[:stream_options] = {include_usage: true}.merge!(params[:stream_options] || {})
218:       end

223:       messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
224:       body = LLM.json.dump({messages: adapt(messages, mode: :complete).flatten}.merge!(params))

226:       transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))

3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # {LLM::Response LLM::Response} is the normalized base shape for
6:   # provider and endpoint responses in llm.rb.
7:   #
8:   # Provider calls return an instance of this class, then extend it
9:   # with provider-, endpoint-, or context-specific modules so response
10:   # handling can share one common surface without flattening away
11:   # specialized behavior.
12:   #
13:   # The normalized response keeps the transport response available
14:   # through {#res}. When the default net/http transport is in use,
15:   # {LLM::Transport::Response::HTTP
16:   # LLM::Transport::Response::HTTP} keeps the
17:   # original {Net::HTTPResponse Net::HTTPResponse} available through
18:   # its own {LLM::Transport::Response::HTTP#res #res}.
19:   class Response
20:     require "json"
21: 
22:     ##
23:     # Returns the HTTP response
24:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
25:     attr_reader :res
26: 
27:     ##
28:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response] res
29:     #  HTTP response
30:     # @return [LLM::Response]
31:     #  Returns an instance of LLM::Response
32:     def initialize(res)
33:       @res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
34:     end
35: 
36:     ##
37:     # Returns the response body
38:     # @return [LLM::Object, String]
39:     #  Returns an LLM::Object when the response body is JSON,
40:     #  otherwise returns a raw string.
41:     def body
42:       @res.body
43:     end
44: 
45:     ##
46:     # Returns an inspection of the response object
47:     # @return [String]
48:     def inspect
49:       "#<#{LLM::Utils.object_id(self)} @body=#{body.inspect} @res=#{@res.inspect}>"
50:     end
51: 
52:     ##
53:     # Returns true if the response is successful
54:     # @return [Boolean]
55:     def ok?
56:       @res.success?
57:     end
58: 
59:     ##
60:     # Returns true if the response is from the Files API
61:     # @return [Boolean]
62:     def file?
63:       false
64:     end
65: 
66:     private
67: 
68:     def method_missing(m, *args, **kwargs, &b)
69:       if LLM::Object === body
70:         body.respond_to?(m) ? body[m.to_s] : super
71:       else
72:         super
73:       end
74:     end
75: 
76:     def respond_to_missing?(m, include_private = false)
77:       if LLM::Object === body
78:         body.respond_to?(m)
79:       else
80:         false
81:       end
82:     end
83:   end
84: end

3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # The Anthropic class implements a provider for
6:   # [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com).
7:   #
8:   # @example
9:   #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
10:   #   require "llm"
11:   #
12:   #   llm = LLM.anthropic(key: ENV["KEY"])
13:   #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
14:   #   ctx.talk ["Tell me about this photo", ctx.local_file("/images/photo.png")]
15:   #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { print "[#{_1.role}]", _1.content, "\n" }
16:   class Anthropic < Provider
17:     require_relative "anthropic/utils"
18:     require_relative "anthropic/error_handler"
19:     require_relative "anthropic/request_adapter"
20:     require_relative "anthropic/response_adapter"
21:     require_relative "anthropic/stream_parser"
22:     require_relative "anthropic/models"
23:     require_relative "anthropic/files"
24:     include RequestAdapter
25:     extend Utils
26: 
27:     HOST = "api.anthropic.com"
28: 
29:     ##
30:     # @param key (see LLM::Provider#initialize)
31:     def initialize(**)
32:       super(host: HOST, **)
33:     end
34: 
35:     ##
36:     # @return [Symbol]
37:     #  Returns the provider's name
38:     def name
39:       :anthropic
40:     end
41: 
42:     ##
43:     # Provides an interface to the chat completions API
44:     # @see https://docs.anthropic.com/en/api/messages Anthropic docs
45:     # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
46:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
47:     # @example (see LLM::Provider#complete)
48:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
49:     # @raise [LLM::PromptError]
50:     #  When given an object a provider does not understand
51:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#complete)
52:     def complete(prompt, params = {})
53:       params, stream, tools, role = normalize_complete_params(params)
54:       req = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
55:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream: stream, operation: "chat", model: params[:model])
56:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
57:         .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
58:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model: params[:model], res:, span:)
59:       res
60:     end
61: 
62:     ##
63:     # Provides an interface to Anthropic's models API
64:     # @see https://docs.anthropic.com/en/api/models-list
65:     # @return [LLM::Anthropic::Models]

68:     end

72:     # @see https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/files Anthropic docs

74:     def files
75:       LLM::Anthropic::Files.new(self)
76:     end

80:     def assistant_role
81:       "assistant"
82:     end
83:

86:     # containing `tool_result` content blocks rather than a distinct
87:     # `tool` role.
88:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#tool_role)
89:     def tool_role

91:     end
92:

96:     # @return [String]

104:     #  through a set of options that are easier to set through the
105:     #  {LLM::Provider#server_tool LLM::Provider#server_tool} method.

112:         text_editor: server_tool(:str_replace_based_edit_tool, type: "text_editor_20250728", max_characters: 10_000)

3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # The Google class implements a provider for
6:   # [Gemini](https://ai.google.dev/). The Google provider
7:   # can accept multiple inputs (text, images, audio, and video).
8:   # The inputs can be provided inline via the prompt for files
9:   # under 20MB or via the Gemini Files API for files
10:   # that are over 20MB.
11:   #
12:   # @example
13:   #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
14:   #   require "llm"
15:   #
16:   #   llm = LLM.google(key: ENV["KEY"])
17:   #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
18:   #   ctx.talk ["Tell me about this photo", ctx.local_file("/images/photo.png")]
19:   #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { print "[#{_1.role}]", _1.content, "\n" }
20:   class Google < Provider
21:     require_relative "google/utils"
22:     require_relative "google/error_handler"
23:     require_relative "google/request_adapter"
24:     require_relative "google/response_adapter"
25:     require_relative "google/stream_parser"
26:     require_relative "google/models"
27:     require_relative "google/images"
28:     require_relative "google/audio"
29:     require_relative "google/files"
30: 
31:     include RequestAdapter
32:     extend Utils
33: 
34:     HOST = "generativelanguage.googleapis.com"
35: 
36:     ##
37:     # @param key (see LLM::Provider#initialize)
38:     def initialize(**)
39:       super(host: HOST, **)
40:     end
41: 
42:     ##
43:     # @return [Symbol]
44:     #  Returns the provider's name
45:     def name
46:       :google
47:     end
48: 
49:     ##
50:     # Provides an embedding
51:     # @param input (see LLM::Provider#embed)
52:     # @param model (see LLM::Provider#embed)
53:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#embed)
54:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
55:     # @return [LLM::Response]
56:     def embed(input, model: "gemini-embedding-001", **params)
57:       model = model.respond_to?(:id) ? model.id : model
58:       path = ["/v1beta/models/#{model}", "embedContent?key=#{@key}"].join(":")
59:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.post(path, headers)
60:       req.body = LLM.json.dump({content: {parts: [{text: input}]}})
61:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, operation: "embeddings", model:)
62:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :embedding)
63:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "embeddings", model:, res:, span:)
64:       res
65:     end
66: 
67:     ##
68:     # Provides an interface to the chat completions API
69:     # @see https://ai.google.dev/api/generate-content#v1beta.models.generateContent Gemini docs
70:     # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
71:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
72:     # @example (see LLM::Provider#complete)

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::OpenAI
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class StreamParser
7:     EMPTY_HASH = {}.freeze
8: 
9:     ##
10:     # Returns the fully constructed response body
11:     # @return [Hash]
12:     attr_reader :body
13: 
14:     ##
15:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::Chunk]
16:     def initialize(stream)
17:       @body = {}
18:       @stream = stream
19:       @emits = {tools: {}}
20:       @can_emit_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_content)
21:       @can_emit_reasoning_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_reasoning_content)
22:       @can_emit_tool_call = stream.respond_to?(:on_tool_call)
23:       @can_push_content = stream.respond_to?(:<<)
24:     end
25: 
26:     ##
27:     # @param [Hash] chunk
28:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::Chunk]
29:     def parse!(chunk)
30:       tap { merge!(chunk) }
31:     end
32: 
33:     ##
34:     # Frees internal parser state used during streaming.
35:     # @return [void]
36:     def free
37:       @emits.clear
38:     end
39: 
40:     private
41: 
42:     def merge!(chunk)
43:       chunk.each do |key, value|
44:         if key == "choices"
45:           @body["choices"] ||= []
46:           merge_choices!(value)
47:         else
48:           @body[key] = value
49:         end
50:       end
51:     end
52: 
53:     def merge_choices!(choices)
54:       body_choices = @body["choices"]
55:       choices.each do |choice|
56:         index = choice["index"]
57:         delta = choice["delta"] || EMPTY_HASH
58:         target_message = if body_choice = body_choices[index]
59:           body_choice["message"]
60:         else
61:           body_choices[index] = {"message" => {"role" => "assistant"}}
62:           body_choices[index]["message"]
63:         end
64:         merge_delta!(target_message, delta)
65:       end
66:     end
67: 
68:     def merge_delta!(target_message, delta)
69:       if delta.length == 1
70:         merge_single_delta!(target_message, delta)
71:       elsif content = delta["content"]
72:         if target_content = target_message["content"]
73:           target_content << content
74:         else
75:           target_message["content"] = content
76:         end
77:         emit_content(content)
78:       elsif reasoning = delta["reasoning_content"]
79:         if target_reasoning = target_message["reasoning_content"]
80:           target_reasoning << reasoning
81:         else
82:           target_message["reasoning_content"] = reasoning
83:         end
84:         emit_reasoning_content(reasoning)
85:       elsif tool_calls = delta["tool_calls"]
86:         merge_tools!(target_message, tool_calls)
87:       end
88:       return if delta.length <= 1
89:       delta.each do |key, value|
90:         next if value.nil? || key == "content" || key == "reasoning_content" || key == "tool_calls"
91:         target_message[key] = value
92:       end
93:     end
94: 
95:     def merge_single_delta!(target_message, delta)
96:       if content = delta["content"]
97:         if target_content = target_message["content"]
98:           target_content << content
99:         else
100:           target_message["content"] = content
101:         end
102:         emit_content(content)
103:         return
104:       end
105:       if reasoning = delta["reasoning_content"]
106:         if target_reasoning = target_message["reasoning_content"]
107:           target_reasoning << reasoning
108:         else
109:           target_message["reasoning_content"] = reasoning
110:         end
111:         emit_reasoning_content(reasoning)
112:         return
113:       end
114:       if tool_calls = delta["tool_calls"]
115:         merge_tools!(target_message, tool_calls)
116:       end
117:     end
118: 
119:     def merge_tools!(target, tools)
120:       target["tool_calls"] ||= []
121:       tools.each.with_index do |toola, index|
122:         tindex = toola["index"]
123:         tindex = index unless Integer === tindex && tindex >= 0
124:         toolb = target["tool_calls"][tindex]
125:         functiona = toola["function"]
126:         functionb = toolb && toolb["function"]
127:         if functiona && functionb
128:           # Append to existing function arguments
129:           merge_function!(functionb, functiona)
130:         else
131:           target["tool_calls"][tindex] = toola
132:         end
133:         emit_tool(target["tool_calls"][tindex], tindex)
134:       end
135:     end
136: 
137:     def merge_function!(target, source)
138:       if arguments = source["arguments"]
139:         if target_arguments = target["arguments"]
140:           target_arguments << arguments
141:         else
142:           target["arguments"] = arguments
143:         end
144:       end
145:       if name = source["name"]
146:         if target_name = target["name"]
147:           target_name << name
148:         else
149:           target["name"] = name
150:         end
151:       end
152:       return if source.length <= 2
153:       source.each do |func_key, func_value|
154:         next if func_key == "arguments" || func_key == "name"
155:         target[func_key] ||= +""
156:         target[func_key] << func_value
157:       end
158:     end
159: 
160:     def emit_content(value)
161:       if @can_emit_content
162:         @stream.on_content(value)
163:       elsif @can_push_content
164:         @stream << value
165:       end
166:     end
167: 
168:     def emit_reasoning_content(value)
169:       if @can_emit_reasoning_content
170:         @stream.on_reasoning_content(value)
171:       end
172:     end
173: 
174:     def emit_tool(tool, tindex)
175:       return unless @can_emit_tool_call
176:       return if @emits[:tools][tindex]
177:       function = tool["function"]
178:       return unless function && tool["id"] && function["name"]
179:       return unless arguments_complete?(function["arguments"])
180:       arguments = parse_arguments(function["arguments"])
181:       return unless arguments
182:       function, error = resolve_tool(tool, function, arguments)
183:       @emits[:tools][tindex] = true
184:       @stream.on_tool_call(function, error)
185:     end
186: 
187:     def resolve_tool(tool, function, arguments)
188:       registered = @stream.__find__(function["name"])
189:       fn = (registered || LLM::Function.new(function["name"])).dup.tap do |fn|
190:         fn.id = tool["id"]
191:         fn.arguments = arguments
192:         fn.tracer = @stream.extra[:tracer]
193:         fn.model = @stream.extra[:model]
194:       end
195:       [fn, (registered ? nil : fn.unavailable)]
196:     end
197: 
198:     def parse_arguments(arguments)
199:       return nil if arguments.to_s.empty?
200:       parsed = LLM.json.load(arguments)
201:       Hash === parsed ? parsed : nil
202:     rescue *LLM.json.parser_error
203:       nil
204:     end
205: 
206:     def arguments_complete?(arguments)
207:       value = arguments.to_s.rstrip
208:       !value.empty? && value.end_with?("}")
209:     end
210:   end
211: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::OpenAI
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class ErrorHandler
7:     ##
8:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
9:     #  Non-2XX response from the server
10:     attr_reader :res
11: 
12:     ##
13:     # @return [Object, nil]
14:     #  The span
15:     attr_reader :span
16: 
17:     ##
18:     # @param [LLM::Tracer] tracer
19:     #  The tracer
20:     # @param [Object, nil] span
21:     #  The span
22:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
23:     #  The response from the server
24:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::ErrorHandler]
25:     def initialize(tracer, span, res)
26:       @tracer = tracer
27:       @span = span
28:       @res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
29:     end
30: 
31:     ##
32:     # @raise [LLM::Error]
33:     #  Raises a subclass of {LLM::Error LLM::Error}
34:     def raise_error!
35:       ex = error

68:         end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Anthropic
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class StreamParser
7:     ##
8:     # Returns the fully constructed response body
9:     # @return [Hash]
10:     attr_reader :body
11: 
12:     ##
13:     # @param [#<<, LLM::Stream] stream
14:     #  A stream sink that implements {#<<} or the {LLM::Stream} interface
15:     # @return [LLM::Anthropic::StreamParser]
16:     def initialize(stream)
17:       @body = {"role" => "assistant", "content" => []}
18:       @stream = stream
19:       @can_emit_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_content)
20:       @can_emit_tool_call = stream.respond_to?(:on_tool_call)
21:       @can_push_content = stream.respond_to?(:<<)
22:     end
23: 
24:     ##
25:     # @param [Hash] chunk
26:     # @return [LLM::Anthropic::StreamParser]
27:     def parse!(chunk)
28:       tap { merge!(chunk) }
29:     end
30: 
31:     ##
32:     # Frees internal parser state used during streaming.
33:     # @return [void]
34:     def free
35:     end
36: 
37:     private
38: 
39:     def merge!(chunk)
40:       if chunk["type"] == "message_start"
41:         merge_message!(chunk["message"])
42:       elsif chunk["type"] == "content_block_start"
43:         @body["content"][chunk["index"]] = chunk["content_block"]
44:       elsif chunk["type"] == "content_block_delta"
45:         if chunk["delta"]["type"] == "text_delta"
46:           @body["content"][chunk["index"]]["text"] << chunk["delta"]["text"]
47:           emit_content(chunk["delta"]["text"])
48:         elsif chunk["delta"]["type"] == "input_json_delta"
49:           content = @body["content"][chunk["index"]]
50:           if Hash === content["input"]
51:             content["input"] = chunk["delta"]["partial_json"]
52:           else
53:             content["input"] ||= +""
54:             content["input"] << chunk["delta"]["partial_json"]
55:           end
56:         end
57:       elsif chunk["type"] == "message_delta"
58:         merge_message!(chunk["delta"]) if chunk["delta"]
59:         extras = chunk.reject { |k, _| k == "type" || k == "delta" }
60:         merge_message!(extras) unless extras.empty?
61:       elsif chunk["type"] == "content_block_stop"
62:         content = @body["content"][chunk["index"]]
63:         if content["input"]
64:           content["input"] = LLM.json.load(content["input"])
65:         end
66:         if content["type"] == "tool_use"
67:           emit_tool(content)
68:         end
69:       end
70:     end
71: 
72:     def merge_message!(message)
73:       message.each_pair do |key, value|
74:         if value.respond_to?(:each_pair)
75:           @body[key] ||= {}
76:           deep_merge!(@body[key], value)
77:         else
78:           @body[key] = value
79:         end
80:       end
81:     end
82: 
83:     def deep_merge!(target, source)
84:       source.each_pair do |key, value|
85:         if value.respond_to?(:each_pair) && target[key].respond_to?(:each_pair)
86:           deep_merge!(target[key], value)
87:         else
88:           target[key] = value
89:         end
90:       end
91:     end
92: 
93:     def emit_content(value)
94:       if @can_emit_content
95:         @stream.on_content(value)
96:       elsif @can_push_content
97:         @stream << value
98:       end
99:     end
100: 
101:     def emit_tool(tool)
102:       return unless @can_emit_tool_call
103:       function, error = resolve_tool(tool)
104:       @stream.on_tool_call(function, error)
105:     end
106: 
107:     def resolve_tool(tool)
108:       registered = @stream.__find__(tool["name"])
109:       fn = (registered || LLM::Function.new(tool["name"])).dup.tap do |fn|
110:         fn.id = tool["id"]
111:         fn.arguments = LLM::Anthropic.parse_tool_input(tool["input"])
112:         fn.tracer = @stream.extra[:tracer]
113:         fn.model = @stream.extra[:model]
114:       end
115:       [fn, (registered ? nil : fn.unavailable)]
116:     end
117:   end
118: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Google
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class StreamParser
7:     ##
8:     # Returns the fully constructed response body
9:     # @return [Hash]
10:     attr_reader :body
11: 
12:     ##
13:     # @param [#<<, LLM::Stream] stream
14:     #  A stream sink that implements {#<<} or the {LLM::Stream} interface
15:     # @return [LLM::Google::StreamParser]
16:     def initialize(stream)
17:       @body = {"candidates" => []}
18:       @stream = stream
19:       @emits = {tools: []}
20:       @can_emit_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_content)
21:       @can_emit_tool_call = stream.respond_to?(:on_tool_call)
22:       @can_push_content = stream.respond_to?(:<<)
23:     end
24: 
25:     ##
26:     # @param [Hash] chunk
27:     # @return [LLM::Google::StreamParser]
28:     def parse!(chunk)
29:       tap { merge_chunk!(chunk) }
30:     end
31: 
32:     ##
33:     # Frees internal parser state used during streaming.
34:     # @return [void]
35:     def free
36:       @emits.clear
37:     end
38: 
39:     private
40: 
41:     def merge_chunk!(chunk)
42:       chunk.each do |key, value|
43:         k = key.to_s
44:         if k == "candidates"
45:           merge_candidates!(value)
46:         elsif k == "usageMetadata" &&
47:             @body["usageMetadata"].is_a?(Hash) &&
48:             value.is_a?(Hash)
49:           @body["usageMetadata"] = @body["usageMetadata"].merge(value)
50:         else
51:           @body[k] = value
52:         end
53:       end
54:     end
55: 
56:     def merge_candidates!(deltas)
57:       deltas.each do |delta|
58:         index = delta["index"].to_i
59:         @body["candidates"][index] ||= {"content" => {"parts" => []}}
60:         candidate = @body["candidates"][index]
61:         delta.each do |key, value|
62:           k = key.to_s
63:           if k == "content"
64:             merge_candidate_content!(candidate["content"], value, index) if value
65:           else
66:             candidate[k] = value # Overwrite other fields
67:           end
68:         end
69:       end
70:     end
71: 
72:     def merge_candidate_content!(content, delta, cindex)
73:       delta.each do |key, value|
74:         k = key.to_s
75:         if k == "parts"
76:           content["parts"] ||= []
77:           merge_content_parts!(content["parts"], value, cindex) if value
78:         else
79:           content[k] = value
80:         end
81:       end
82:     end
83: 
84:     def merge_content_parts!(parts, deltas, cindex)
85:       deltas.each do |delta|
86:         if delta["text"]
87:           merge_text!(parts, delta)
88:         elsif delta["functionCall"]
89:           merge_function_call!(parts, delta, cindex)
90:         elsif delta["inlineData"]
91:           parts << delta
92:         elsif delta["functionResponse"]
93:           parts << delta
94:         elsif delta["fileData"]
95:           parts << delta
96:         else
97:           parts << delta
98:         end
99:       end
100:     end
101: 
102:     def merge_text!(parts, delta)
103:       last_existing_part = parts.last
104:       text = delta["text"]
105:       if last_existing_part.is_a?(Hash) && last_existing_part["text"]
106:         last_existing_part["text"] ||= +""
107:         last_existing_part["text"] << text
108:         emit_content(text)
109:       else
110:         parts << delta
111:         emit_content(text)
112:       end
113:     end
114: 
115:     def merge_function_call!(parts, delta, cindex)
116:       last_existing_part = parts.last
117:       last_call = last_existing_part.is_a?(Hash) ? last_existing_part["functionCall"] : nil
118:       delta_call = delta["functionCall"]
119:       if last_call.is_a?(Hash) && delta_call.is_a?(Hash)
120:         last_existing_part["functionCall"] = last_call.merge(delta_call)
121:         delta.each do |key, value|
122:           next if key == "functionCall"
123:           last_existing_part[key] = value
124:         end
125:       else
126:         parts << delta
127:       end
128:       emit_tool(parts.length - 1, cindex, parts.last || delta)
129:     end
130: 
131:     def emit_content(value)
132:       if @can_emit_content
133:         @stream.on_content(value)
134:       elsif @can_push_content
135:         @stream << value
136:       end
137:     end
138: 
139:     def emit_tool(pindex, cindex, part)
140:       return unless @can_emit_tool_call
141:       return unless complete_tool?(part)
142:       key = [cindex, pindex]
143:       return if @emits[:tools].include?(key)
144:       function, error = resolve_tool(part, cindex, pindex)
145:       @emits[:tools] << key
146:       @stream.on_tool_call(function, error)
147:     end
148: 
149:     def complete_tool?(part)
150:       call = part["functionCall"]
151:       call && call["name"] && Hash === call["args"]
152:     end
153: 
154:     def resolve_tool(part, cindex, pindex)
155:       call = part["functionCall"]
156:       registered = @stream.__find__(call["name"])
157:       fn = (registered || LLM::Function.new(call["name"])).dup.tap do |fn|
158:         fn.id = LLM::Google.tool_id(part:, cindex:, pindex:)
159:         fn.arguments = call["args"]
160:         fn.tracer = @stream.extra[:tracer]
161:         fn.model = @stream.extra[:model]
162:       end
163:       [fn, (registered ? nil : fn.unavailable)]
164:     end
165:   end
166: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # The Bedrock class implements a provider for
6:   # [Amazon Bedrock](https://aws.amazon.com/bedrock/).
7:   #
8:   # Bedrock provides access to foundation models from Anthropic, Meta,
9:   # Mistral, AI21 Labs, Cohere, and more through the AWS infrastructure.
10:   # This provider uses the Bedrock Converse API for chat completions,
11:   # and the Converse Stream API for streaming.
12:   #
13:   # Unlike other llm.rb providers which use API key authentication,
14:   # Bedrock uses AWS Signature V4 (SigV4) for request signing.
15:   # You must provide AWS credentials (access key, secret key, and region)
16:   # instead of a single API key.
17:   #
18:   # Streaming uses the AWS Event Stream binary protocol instead of
19:   # standard SSE. The binary framing is decoded inline using only
20:   # Ruby's stdlib.
21:   #
22:   # @example
23:   #   require "llm"
24:   #
25:   #   llm = LLM.bedrock(
26:   #     access_key_id: ENV["AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID"],
27:   #     secret_access_key: ENV["AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY"],
28:   #     region: "us-east-1"
29:   #   )
30:   #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
31:   #   ctx.talk "Hello, how are you?"
32:   #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { puts _1.content }
33:   class Bedrock < Provider
34:     require_relative "bedrock/signature"
35:     require_relative "bedrock/error_handler"
36:     require_relative "bedrock/request_adapter"
37:     require_relative "bedrock/request_adapter/completion"
38:     require_relative "bedrock/response_adapter"
39:     require_relative "bedrock/response_adapter/completion"
40:     require_relative "bedrock/response_adapter/models"
41:     require_relative "bedrock/stream_decoder"
42:     require_relative "bedrock/stream_parser"
43:     require_relative "bedrock/models"
44: 
45:     include RequestAdapter
46: 
47:     HOST_PATTERN = "bedrock-runtime.%s.amazonaws.com"
48: 
49:     ##
50:     # @param [String] access_key_id AWS access key ID
51:     # @param [String] secret_access_key AWS secret access key

53:     # @param [String, nil] session_token AWS session token for temporary credentials
54:     # @param [String, nil] host Override the Bedrock API host
55:     # @param [Integer] port Connection port

57:     # @param [Integer] timeout Request timeout in seconds
58:     def initialize(access_key_id: nil, secret_access_key: nil,
59:                    region: nil, session_token: nil,
60:                    host: nil, port: 443, ssl: true, timeout: 60,
61:                    **)

63:       @access_key_id = access_key_id
64:       @secret_access_key = secret_access_key
65:       @aws_region = region

87:       params, stream, tools, role = normalize_complete_params(params)
88:       req, messages, body = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role, stream:)
89:       tracer.set_request_metadata(user_input: extract_user_input(messages, fallback: prompt))
90:       sign!(req, body)
91:       model_id = model_id_for(req.path)
92:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream:, operation: "chat", stream_parser:, model: model_id)
93:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
94:         .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
95:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model: model_id, res:, span:)
96:       res

204:     def normalize_complete_params(params)
205:       params = {role: :user, model: default_model, max_tokens: 2048}.merge!(params)
206:       tools = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
207:       params = [params, adapt_schema(params), adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
208:       role, stream = params.delete(:role), params.delete(:stream)
209:       params[:stream] = true if streamable?(stream) || stream == true
210:       [params, stream, tools, role]
211:     end
212: 
213:     def build_complete_request(prompt, params, role, stream: nil)
214:       messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
215:       model_id = params.delete(:model) || default_model
216:       payload = build_converse_payload(messages, params)
217:       body = LLM.json.dump(payload)
218:       path = stream ? "/model/#{model_id}/converse-stream" \
219:                     : "/model/#{model_id}/converse"
220:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.post(path, headers)
221:       transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))
222:       [req, messages, body]
223:     end

225:     def build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
226:       if LLM::Prompt === prompt
227:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), *prompt]
228:       else
229:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), Message.new(role, prompt)]
230:       end
231:     end

233:     def build_converse_payload(messages, params)
234:       adapted = adapt(messages)
235:       payload = {}
236:       payload[:system] = adapted[:system] if adapted[:system]&.any?
237:       payload[:messages] = adapted[:messages]
238:       inference_config = {}
239:       inference_config[:maxTokens] = params.delete(:max_tokens) if params[:max_tokens]
240:       inference_config[:temperature] = params.delete(:temperature) if params.key?(:temperature)
241:       inference_config[:topP] = params.delete(:top_p) if params.key?(:top_p)
242:       inference_config[:stopSequences] = params.delete(:stop) if params[:stop]
243:       payload[:inferenceConfig] = inference_config unless inference_config.empty?
244:       payload[:toolConfig] = params.delete(:toolConfig) if params[:toolConfig]
245:       payload[:outputConfig] = params.delete(:outputConfig) if params[:outputConfig]
246:       additional = {}
247:       top_k = params.delete(:top_k)
248:       additional[:top_k] = top_k if top_k
249:       payload[:additionalModelRequestFields] = additional unless additional.empty?
250:       payload
251:     end
252: 
253:     def extract_user_input(messages, fallback:)
254:       message = messages.reverse.find(&:user?) || messages.last
255:       value = message&.content || fallback
256:       value.is_a?(String) ? value : LLM.json.dump(value)
257:     end
258: 
259:     def model_id_for(path)
260:       path[%r{\A/model/(.+?)/converse(?:-stream)?\z}, 1] || default_model
261:     end

263:     def sign!(req, body)
264:       Signature.new(
265:         credentials:,
266:         method: req.method,
267:         path: req.path,
268:         body:
269:       ).sign!(req)
270:     end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true

3: class LLM::Anthropic
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   module RequestAdapter
7:     require_relative "request_adapter/completion"
8: 
9:     ##
10:     # @param [Array<LLM::Message>] messages
11:     #  The messages to adapt
12:     # @return [Hash]
13:     def adapt(messages, mode: nil)
14:       payload = {messages: [], system: []}
15:       messages.each do |message|
16:         adapted = Completion.new(message).adapt
17:         next if adapted.nil?
18:         if system?(message)
19:           payload[:system].concat Array(adapted[:content])
20:         else
21:           payload[:messages] << adapted
22:         end
23:       end
24:       payload.delete(:system) if payload[:system].empty?
25:       payload
26:     end
27: 
28:     private
29: 
30:     ##
31:     # @param [Hash] params
32:     # @return [Hash]
33:     def adapt_tools(tools)
34:       return {} unless tools&.any?
35:       {tools: tools.map { _1.respond_to?(:adapt) ? _1.adapt(self) : _1 }}
36:     end
37: 
38:     def system?(message)
39:       if message.respond_to?(:system?)
40:         message.system?
41:       else
42:         Hash === message and message[:role].to_s == "system"
43:       end
44:     end
45:   end
46: end

4:   ##

7:     require_relative "response_adapter/completion"
8:     require_relative "response_adapter/enumerable"
9:     require_relative "response_adapter/file"

13:     module_function
14: 
15:     ##
16:     # @param [LLM::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
17:     # @param [Symbol] type
18:     # @return [LLM::Response]
19:     def adapt(res, type:)
20:       response = (LLM::Response === res) ? res : LLM::Response.new(res)
21:       response.extend(select(type))
22:     end
23: 
24:     ##
25:     # @api private
26:     def select(type)
27:       case type
28:       when :completion then LLM::Anthropic::ResponseAdapter::Completion
29:       when :enumerable then LLM::Anthropic::ResponseAdapter::Enumerable
30:       when :file then LLM::Anthropic::ResponseAdapter::File
31:       when :models then LLM::Anthropic::ResponseAdapter::Models
32:       when :web_search then LLM::Anthropic::ResponseAdapter::WebSearch
33:       else
34:         raise ArgumentError, "Unknown response adapter type: #{type.inspect}"
35:       end
36:     end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Bedrock
4:   ##
5:   # Adapts llm.rb internal message format to Bedrock Converse API format.
6:   #
7:   # Bedrock Converse uses:
8:   #   - system: [{text: "..."}]  (top-level, separate from messages)
9:   #   - messages: [{role: "user"|"assistant", content: [{...}, ...]}]
10:   #   - Content blocks: text, image, document, toolUse, toolResult
11:   #   - toolConfig: {tools: [{toolSpec: {name:, description:, inputSchema: {json: ...}}}]}
12:   #
13:   # @api private
14:   module RequestAdapter
15:     ##
16:     # @param [Array<LLM::Message>] messages
17:     # @return [Hash]
18:     def adapt(messages, mode: nil)
19:       payload = {messages: [], system: []}
20:       messages.each do |message|
21:         adapted = Completion.new(message).adapt
22:         next if adapted.nil?
23:         if system?(message)
24:           payload[:system].concat Array(adapted[:content])
25:         else
26:           payload[:messages] << adapted
27:         end
28:       end
29:       payload.delete(:system) if payload[:system].empty?
30:       payload
31:     end
32: 
33:     private
34: 
35:     ##
36:     # @param [Hash] params
37:     # @return [Hash]
38:     def adapt_schema(params)
39:       return {} unless params&.key?(:schema)
40:       schema = params.delete(:schema)
41:       schema = schema.respond_to?(:object) ? schema.object : schema
42:       cleaned = schema.respond_to?(:to_h) ? schema.to_h : schema
43:       [:strict, "strict", :$schema, "$schema"].each { cleaned.delete(_1) }
44:       {
45:         outputConfig: {
46:           textFormat: {
47:             type: "json_schema",
48:             structure: {
49:               jsonSchema: {
50:                 name: "response",
51:                 schema: LLM.json.dump(cleaned)
52:               }
53:             }
54:           }
55:         }
56:       }
57:     end
58: 
59:     ##
60:     # @param [Array<LLM::Function>] tools
61:     # @return [Hash]
62:     def adapt_tools(tools)
63:       return {} unless tools&.any?
64:       {toolConfig: {tools: tools.map { |t| adapt_tool(t) }}}
65:     end
66: 
67:     ##
68:     # @param [LLM::Function] tool
69:     # @return [Hash]
70:     def adapt_tool(tool)
71:       function = tool.respond_to?(:function) ? tool.function : tool
72:       {
73:         toolSpec: {
74:           name: function.name,
75:           description: function.description,
76:           inputSchema: {
77:             json: function.params || default_input_schema
78:           }
79:         }
80:       }
81:     end
82: 
83:     def default_input_schema
84:       {"type" => "object", "properties" => {}, "required" => []}
85:     end
86: 
87:     def system?(message)
88:       if message.respond_to?(:system?)
89:         message.system?
90:       else
91:         Hash === message && message[:role].to_s == "system"
92:       end
93:     end
94:   end
95: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require "digest"
4: 
5: class LLM::Bedrock
6:   ##
7:   # Decodes AWS Event Stream binary frames.
8:   #
9:   # Bedrock Converse Stream uses the AWS Event Stream protocol,
10:   # a binary framing format (not SSE). Each message has:
11:   #   - total length (4 bytes, big-endian)
12:   #   - headers length (4 bytes, big-endian)
13:   #   - prelude CRC (4 bytes)
14:   #   - headers (variable)
15:   #   - payload (variable, usually JSON)
16:   #   - message CRC (4 bytes)
17:   #
18:   # Implements #<< to match the interface expected by llm.rb's
19:   # streaming transport, so it can replace the SSE-based
20:   # StreamDecoder when streaming from Bedrock.
21:   #
22:   # @api private
23:   class StreamDecoder
24:     ##
25:     # @return [LLM::Bedrock::StreamParser]
26:     attr_reader :parser
27: 
28:     ##
29:     # @param [LLM::Bedrock::StreamParser] parser
30:     def initialize(parser)
31:       @buffer = +"".b
32:       @parser = parser
33:     end
34: 
35:     ##
36:     # Feeds a raw binary chunk into the decoder.
37:     # Accumulates data until complete frames are available,
38:     # then decodes them and passes the JSON payload to the parser.
39:     #
40:     # @param [String] chunk Raw binary data
41:     # @return [void]
42:     def <<(chunk)
43:       @buffer << chunk
44:       decode_frames
45:     end
46: 
47:     ##
48:     # @return [Hash] The fully constructed response body
49:     def body
50:       parser.body
51:     end
52: 
53:     ##
54:     # @return [void]
55:     def free
56:       @buffer.clear
57:       parser.free if parser.respond_to?(:free)
58:     end
59: 
60:     private
61: 
62:     def decode_frames
63:       loop do
64:         break if @buffer.bytesize < 12
65:         total_length = @buffer[0, 4].unpack1("N")
66:         break if @buffer.bytesize < total_length
67:         # headers_length = @buffer[4, 4].unpack1("N")
68:         # prelude_crc = @buffer[8, 4].unpack1("N")
69:         headers = decode_headers
70:         payload_start = 12 + headers[:length]
71:         payload_length = total_length - payload_start - 4
72:         payload = @buffer[payload_start, payload_length] if payload_length > 0
73:         # message_crc from last 4 bytes, not needed for our purposes
74:         json = payload ? LLM.json.load(payload) : {}
75:         parser.parse!(json, event_type: headers[:event_type]) if json.is_a?(Hash)
76:         @buffer = @buffer[total_length..] || +"".b
77:       end
78:     end
79: 
80:     def decode_headers
81:       headers_length = @buffer[4, 4].unpack1("N")
82:       offset = 12
83:       end_offset = offset + headers_length
84:       result = {event_type: nil, length: headers_length}
85:       while offset < end_offset
86:         name_len = @buffer.getbyte(offset)
87:         offset += 1
88:         break if offset + name_len > end_offset
89:         name = @buffer[offset, name_len]
90:         offset += name_len
91:         break if offset >= end_offset
92:         value_type = @buffer.getbyte(offset)
93:         offset += 1
94:         value = case value_type
95:         when 7 # string
96:           str_len = @buffer[offset, 2].unpack1("n")
97:           offset += 2
98:           str = @buffer[offset, str_len]
99:           offset += str_len
100:           str
101:         when 8 # binary
102:           bin_len = @buffer[offset, 2].unpack1("n")
103:           offset += 2
104:           bin = @buffer[offset, bin_len]
105:           offset += bin_len
106:           bin
107:         when 9 # boolean true
108:           true
109:         when 1 # boolean false
110:           false
111:         when 2 # byte
112:           val = @buffer.getbyte(offset)
113:           offset += 1
114:           val
115:         when 3 # int16
116:           val = @buffer[offset, 2].unpack1("s>")
117:           offset += 2
118:           val
119:         when 4 # int32
120:           val = @buffer[offset, 4].unpack1("l>")
121:           offset += 4
122:           val
123:         when 6 # byte array (as raw string)
124:           bin_len = @buffer[offset, 2].unpack1("n")
125:           offset += 2
126:           bin = @buffer[offset, bin_len]
127:           offset += bin_len
128:           bin
129:         else
130:           # Unknown type, skip to end of headers
131:           offset = end_offset
132:           nil
133:         end
134:         result[:event_type] = value if name == ":event-type"
135:         result[name] = value if name
136:       end
137:       result
138:     end
139:   end
140: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Bedrock
4:   ##
5:   # Parses Bedrock Converse Stream events into a response body
6:   # and emits stream callbacks (on_content, on_tool_call, etc.).
7:   #
8:   # Receives decoded JSON payloads from {StreamDecoder} along with
9:   # the AWS Event Stream event type header.
10:   #
11:   # Bedrock Converse Stream event types:
12:   #   messageStart — initial role
13:   #   contentBlockStart — tool use or reasoning start
14:   #   contentBlockDelta — text delta, tool input JSON, or reasoning text
15:   #   contentBlockStop — content block finished
16:   #   messageStop — final stop reason, usage metadata
17:   #
18:   # @api private
19:   class StreamParser
20:     TOOL_MARKER = "<｜DSML｜function_calls"
21: 
22:     ##
23:     # @return [Hash] Fully constructed response body
24:     attr_reader :body
25: 
26:     ##
27:     # @param [#<<, LLM::Stream] stream
28:     def initialize(stream)
29:       @body = {"output" => {"message" => {"role" => "assistant", "content" => []}}}
30:       @stream = stream
31:       @text_markers = {}
32:       @can_emit_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_content)
33:       @can_emit_reasoning_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_reasoning_content)
34:       @can_emit_tool_call = stream.respond_to?(:on_tool_call)
35:       @can_push_content = stream.respond_to?(:<<)
36:     end
37: 
38:     ##
39:     # @param [Hash] payload Decoded JSON from an event stream frame
40:     # @param [String, nil] event_type The :event-type header value
41:     # @return [self]
42:     def parse!(payload, event_type: nil)
43:       type = event_type || payload["type"]
44:       case type
45:       when "messageStart"
46:         # { "role" => "assistant" }
47:       when "contentBlockStart"
48:         # { "contentBlockIndex" => 0, "start" => { "toolUse" => {...} } }
49:         handle_content_block_start(payload)
50:       when "contentBlockDelta"
51:         # { "contentBlockIndex" => 0, "delta" => { "text" => "..." } }
52:         handle_content_block_delta(payload)
53:       when "contentBlockStop"
54:         handle_content_block_stop(payload)
55:       when "messageStop"
56:         # { "stopReason" => "end_turn", "metadata" => {"usage" => {...}} }
57:         merge_metadata(payload)
58:       when "metadata"
59:         # { "usage" => {...} }
60:         merge_metadata(payload)
61:       end
62:       self
63:     end
64: 
65:     ##
66:     # @return [void]
67:     def free
68:       @text_markers.clear
69:     end
70: 
71:     private
72: 
73:     def handle_content_block_start(payload)
74:       index = payload["contentBlockIndex"]
75:       start_data = payload["start"] || {}
76:       if (tool_use = start_data["toolUse"])
77:         content[index] = {"toolUse" => {"toolUseId" => tool_use["toolUseId"], "name" => tool_use["name"], "input" => +""}}
78:       elsif (reasoning = start_data["reasoningContent"])
79:         content[index] = {"reasoningContent" => {"text" => +"", "signature" => reasoning["signature"]}.compact}
80:       end
81:     end
82: 
83:     def handle_content_block_delta(payload)
84:       index = payload["contentBlockIndex"]
85:       delta = payload["delta"] || {}
86:       if (text = delta["text"])
87:         ensure_content_block(index, "text")
88:         visible = filtered_text(index, text)
89:         return if visible.empty?
90:         content[index]["text"] ||= +""
91:         content[index]["text"] << visible
92:         emit_content(visible)
93:       elsif (tool_input = delta.dig("toolUse", "input"))
94:         ensure_content_block(index, "tool_use")
95:         content[index]["toolUse"]["input"] ||= +""
96:         content[index]["toolUse"]["input"] << tool_input
97:       elsif (reasoning = delta["reasoningContent"])
98:         ensure_content_block(index, "reasoning")
99:         if reasoning["text"]
100:           content[index]["reasoningContent"]["text"] ||= +""
101:           content[index]["reasoningContent"]["text"] << reasoning["text"]
102:           emit_reasoning_content(reasoning["text"])
103:         end
104:         if reasoning["signature"]
105:           content[index]["reasoningContent"]["signature"] = reasoning["signature"]
106:         end
107:       end
108:     end
109: 
110:     def handle_content_block_stop(payload)
111:       index = payload["contentBlockIndex"]
112:       item = content[index]
113:       return unless item
114:       flush_text(index, item)
115:       if item["toolUse"] && item["toolUse"]["input"].is_a?(String)
116:         parsed = LLM.json.load(item["toolUse"]["input"])
117:         item["toolUse"]["input"] = parsed.is_a?(Hash) ? parsed : {}
118:         emit_tool(item)
119:       end
120:     rescue *LLM.json.parser_error
121:       item["toolUse"]["input"] = {} if item&.dig("toolUse")
122:     end
123: 
124:     def ensure_content_block(index, type)
125:       content[index] ||= case type
126:       when "tool_use" then {"toolUse" => {"input" => +""}}
127:       when "reasoning" then {"reasoningContent" => {"text" => +""}}
128:       else {}
129:       end
130:     end
131: 
132:     def filtered_text(index, text)
133:       state = (@text_markers[index] ||= +"")
134:       value = state << text
135:       value.gsub!(TOOL_MARKER, "")
136:       keep = marker_prefix_length(value)
137:       @text_markers[index] = keep.zero? ? +"" : value[-keep..]
138:       keep.zero? ? value : value[0...-keep]
139:     end
140: 
141:     def flush_text(index, item)
142:       value = @text_markers.delete(index).to_s
143:       return unless item["text"]
144:       if value.empty?
145:         content[index] = {} if item["text"].empty?
146:       else
147:         item["text"] << value
148:         emit_content(value)
149:       end
150:     end
151: 
152:     def marker_prefix_length(value)
153:       [value.length, TOOL_MARKER.length - 1].min.downto(1) do |length|
154:         return length if TOOL_MARKER.start_with?(value[-length..])
155:       end
156:       0
157:     end
158: 
159:     def merge_metadata(payload)
160:       metadata = payload["metadata"] || payload
161:       return unless metadata.is_a?(Hash)
162:       usage = metadata["usage"]
163:       @body["usage"] = usage if usage
164:       @body["stopReason"] = payload["stopReason"] if payload["stopReason"]
165:     end
166: 
167:     def emit_content(value)
168:       if @can_emit_content
169:         @stream.on_content(value)
170:       elsif @can_push_content
171:         @stream << value
172:       end
173:     end
174: 
175:     def emit_reasoning_content(value)
176:       @stream.on_reasoning_content(value) if @can_emit_reasoning_content
177:     end
178: 
179:     def emit_tool(tool)
180:       return unless @can_emit_tool_call
181:       function, error = resolve_tool(tool)
182:       @stream.on_tool_call(function, error)
183:     end
184: 
185:     def resolve_tool(tool)
186:       payload = tool["toolUse"] || {}
187:       registered = @stream.__find__(payload["name"])
188:       fn = (registered || LLM::Function.new(payload["name"])).dup.tap do |f|
189:         f.id = payload["toolUseId"]
190:         f.arguments = payload["input"] || {}
191:         f.tracer = @stream.extra[:tracer]
192:         f.model = @stream.extra[:model]
193:       end
194:       [fn, registered ? nil : fn.unavailable]
195:     end
196: 
197:     def content
198:       @body["output"]["message"]["content"]
199:     end
200:   end
201: end

## Summary - Step 1: Orient in the codebase

**Project**: llm.rb - A Ruby SDK for interacting with multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Bedrock, Ollama, etc.)

**Code organization**:
- `lib/llm.rb` - Main entry point that defines factory methods (e.g., `LLM.openai`, `LLM.anthropic`)
- `lib/llm/provider.rb` - Abstract base class (`LLM::Provider`) that defines the shared interface
- `lib/llm/providers/` - Individual provider implementations (8 providers: openai, anthropic, google, bedrock, ollama, deepseek, xai, zai, llamacpp)
- `lib/llm/registry.rb` - Model registry that loads provider model data from `data/*.json` files
- `lib/llm/response.rb` - Normalized `LLM::Response` class that adapts provider-specific responses
- `lib/llm/error.rb` - Shared error types (`UnauthorizedError`, `RateLimitError`, `ServerError`, `NotFoundError`, etc.)

**Provider registration**:
- Factory methods call `register_for` internally via `LLM.registry_for` → `Registry.for(name)` which loads provider-specific JSON from `data/`
- New providers must be registered in `lib/llm.rb` with factory methods

**Key locations**:
- Provider base: `/lib/llm/provider.rb:8`
- Factory methods: `/lib/llm.rb:112-181`
- Registry loading: `/lib/llm/registry.rb:18`
- Model data: `/data/*.json` (7 files)
- Response adaptation: `/lib/llm/response.rb:32`

## Summary - Step 2: Trace a chat call

**Chat call flow**:
1. User calls `LLM.openai(key: "...").chat("hello")` → `lib/llm.rb:152`
2. Provider creates instance via `LLM::Provider#chat` → `/lib/llm/provider.rb:103`
3. Creates `LLM::Context` which routes to `LLM::Provider#complete` → `/lib/llm/provider.rb:94`
4. Provider-specific `complete` builds request and calls `execute` → `/lib/llm/providers/openai.rb:73`
5. `execute` calls `transport` to send HTTP request

## Summary - Step 5: Map the streaming and error-handling paths

**Error handling flow**:
1. Provider executes HTTP request via `transport`
2. If HTTP error (non-2xx), calls `error_handler.raise_error!` → `/lib/llm/providers/openai/error_handler.rb:34`
3. `error_handler` maps HTTP status to typed errors:
   - 401 → `LLM::UnauthorizedError` (`/lib/llm/error.rb:28`)
   - 429 → `LLM::RateLimitError` (`/lib/llm/error.rb:32`)
   - 5xx → `LLM::ServerError` (`/lib/llm/error.rb:36`)
   - 404 → `LLM::NotFoundError` (`/lib/llm/error.rb:40`)
   - 400 with context_length_exceeded → `LLM::ContextWindowError` (`/lib/llm/error.rb:56`)
   - Other → `LLM::InvalidRequestError` (`/lib/llm/error.rb:52`)

**Provider-specific error handling**:
- OpenAI: Detailed error parsing from response body (`/lib/llm/providers/openai/error_handler.rb:62`)
- Anthropic: Basic HTTP status mapping only
- Google: Specific handling for API_KEY_INVALID (`/lib/llm/providers/google/error_handler.rb:56`)
- Bedrock: Basic HTTP status mapping only

**Streaming flow**:
1. Request includes `stream: true` flag
2. Transport uses `stream_decoder` (default: `/lib/llm/transport/stream_decoder.rb`)
3. Bedrock uses custom decoder: `/lib/llm/providers/bedrock/stream_decoder.rb:30`
4. Decoder parses chunks and passes to `stream_parser.parse!` → `/lib/llm/providers/openai/stream_parser.rb:29`
5. Stream parser emits callbacks:
   - `stream.on_content(value)` for text (`/lib/llm/providers/openai/stream_parser.rb:160`)
   - `stream.on_tool_call(function, error)` for tool calls (`/lib/llm/providers/openai/stream_parser.rb:174`)
   - `stream.on_reasoning_content(value)` for reasoning (OpenAI-specific, `/lib/llm/providers/openai/stream_parser.rb:168`)

**Provider-specific streaming**:
- OpenAI: Parses JSON streaming (SSE); emits tool calls when arguments complete
- Anthropic: Parses SSE; uses JSON chunking for tool input
- Google: Parses SSE; handles multiple candidates and function calls
- Ollama: Minimal parsing; pushes content to stream
- Bedrock: Custom binary decoder for AWS Event Stream; handles toolUse/toolResult content blocks
